sections.header: 1. Department Overview — Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (AIDS)
sections.text:
Artificial Intelligence (AI) is the process of creating machines to think like humans. AI is a term used to describe the simulation of human intelligence processes in machines. It typically involves sophisticated algorithms that enable computers to perform tasks that normally require human intelligence, such as visual perception, speech recognition and decision making. Data Science is a field of knowledge which applies advanced statistical and mathematical techniques to large datasets in order to extract knowledge from them. Data can be structured like a spreadsheet or unstructured like text or video data. Data science is the application of techniques from statistics, mathematics, computer programming and other fields to solve real-world problems using information technology. Due to the exploration of the Internet, AI and Data Science deliver data-driven solutions using computational principles, methods, and systems for extracting information from data and modern computational systems that reveal capabilities of insight, way of thinking, learning, and action that are typical of human intelligence. B.Tech Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (AIDS) has emerged as one of the most high-growth, dynamic, and lucrative careers in technology since 2015. This course aims at providing core technologies such as artificial intelligence, data mining, and data modeling, and also gives intensive inputs in areas of machine learning and big data analytics. Students gain cross-disciplinary skills across fields such as statistics, computer science, machine learning, and logic. Career opportunities exist in healthcare, business, e-commerce, social networking companies, climatology, biotechnology, genetics, and other important areas. The major focus of this programme is to equip students with statistical, mathematical reasoning, machine learning, knowledge discovery, and visualization skills. The programme also deals with core principles and ethical issues in Data Science and AI, and motivates students to explore cutting-edge applications related to Big Data, Neural Networks, and Deep Learning. Applications like Alexa/Siri, Erica/Sophia, Tesla's driverless car, Chat-bot, etc. are becoming common. AI-powered Robots can take over human life-endangering jobs like firefighting, deep-sea oil drilling, and mining works with high mortality rates.

Career opportunities for AIDS graduates include roles such as Software Engineer, Software Tester, Application Developer, Information and Multimedia Designer, Medical Information Scientist, Technology Developer, IT Specialist, Technical Consultant, Quality Analyst, Information Network Manager, and Data Quality Analyst across Manufacturing, Software, Research, Education, Defense, Banking & Finance, e-Commerce, Transport and Health Science.

This chunk answers what is B.Tech Artificial Intelligence and Data Science at MSAJCE, what is the AIDS programme about at MSAJCE, and what is Artificial Intelligence in the context of MSAJCE AIDS department. It also covers what is Data Science at MSAJCE, what career opportunities are available after AIDS from MSAJCE, and what industries can AIDS graduates from MSAJCE work in. Further topics include what is the focus of the B.Tech AIDS programme at MSAJCE, what real-world AI applications are relevant to the AIDS course at MSAJCE, and what skills will students gain from the AIDS programme at MSAJCE, as well as whether AIDS is a good programme in terms of career growth.

sections.header: 2. B.Tech AIDS Programme Details, Vision, Mission and OBE (PEOs, PSOs, POs)
sections.text:
The B.Tech Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (AIDS) program is a 4-year regular program or a 3-year lateral entry program. The program consists of 8 semesters for regular entry and 6 semesters for lateral entry. The total intake is 30 seats, with 15 government seats and 15 management seats. Eligibility requires a 10+2 system of education with a pass in Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics. The curriculum and syllabus follow the 2021 Regulation. The scope for higher studies includes M.E., M.Tech., and M.B.A.

The vision is to be a centre of excellence for transforming students into proficient Artificial Intelligence and Data Science users through sustainable practices.

The mission includes imparting core fundamental knowledge and necessary skills in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science through innovative teaching and learning methodology. It also includes inculcating critical thinking, ethics, lifelong learning, and creativity needed for industry and society, and cultivating students with all-round competencies for career, higher education, and self-employability.

The program educational objectives include preparing graduates for analysing, designing, developing, and testing software solutions and products with creativity and sustainability. Graduates will be skilled in the use of modern tools for critical problem solving and analyzing industrial and societal requirements, and they will be prepared with managerial and leadership skills for career and starting up own firms.

The program specific outcomes include developing creative solutions by adapting emerging technologies / tools for real-time applications of Industry and applying the acquired knowledge to develop software solutions and innovative mobile apps for various automation applications.

The programme outcomes include applying the knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems. They also include identifying, formulating, reviewing research literature, and analyzing complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles. Design solutions for complex engineering problems and design system components or processes that meet the specified needs with appropriate consideration for public health, safety, cultural, societal, and environmental considerations. Use research-based knowledge and research methods including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data, and synthesis of information to provide valid conclusions. Create, select, and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern engineering and IT tools including prediction and modeling to complex engineering activities. Apply reasoning informed by the contextual knowledge to assess societal, health, safety, legal, and cultural issues and consequent responsibilities relevant to professional engineering practice. Understand the impact of professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts, and demonstrate the knowledge of, and need for sustainable development. Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and responsibilities and norms of the engineering practice. Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse teams, and in multidisciplinary settings. Communicate effectively on complex engineering activities with the engineering community and with society at large. Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of engineering and management principles and apply these to one's own work, and recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability to engage in independent and life-long learning in the broadest context of technological change.

The following data is from the Parameter Details table: The Programme is B.Tech Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (AIDS), the Duration is 4 years (Regular) / 3 years (Lateral Entry), the No. of Semesters are 8 (Regular) / 6 (Lateral Entry), the Total Seats are 30, the Government Quota is 15, the Management Quota is 15, the Eligibility is 10+2 with Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics pass, the Syllabus is 2021 Regulation, and Higher Studies options are M.E. / M.Tech. / M.B.A.

This chunk answers how many seats are available in B.Tech AIDS at MSJCE, what is the duration of B.Tech AIDS at MSJCE, how many government quota seats are in AIDS at MSJCE, what is the eligibility for B.Tech AIDS at MSJCE, what is the vision of the AIDS department at MSJCE, what are the PEOs for AIDS at MSJCE, what are the programme outcomes for AIDS at MSJCE, if lateral entry is available for AIDS at MSJCE, what higher studies can be pursued after B.Tech AIDS from MSJCE, and what regulation does B.Tech AIDS follow at MSJCE.

sections.header: 3. AI and Data Science Growth in India 2025–2030 — Key Drivers and Job Roles
sections.text:
The Artificial Intelligence and Data Science sectors in India are expected to have explosive growth over the next five years. AI-driven solutions and data analytics are driving better decisions across all sectors, and the demand for professionals in these fields is expected to grow significantly.

Increased AI Adoption Across Industries includes Healthcare, Finance, E-commerce, Telecommunications, Automotive, and Manufacturing. AI will be used for Predictive analytics, Personalized services, Data-driven decision-making, and Automation. AI-driven solutions will be used in core business functions such as HR, finance, and customer service. Government Support and Initiatives such as Digital India, National AI Strategy, and the National AI Mission initiatives boost AI research, startups, and adoption across public services. India aims to be a global leader in AI for Smart cities, Healthcare, and Agriculture. There is a rising demand for Data-Driven Decision Making including Customer segmentation, Fraud detection, and Supply chain optimization. There is growth in AI-Powered Startups as AI startups are expected to double in the next few years, creating job opportunities for AI engineers, data scientists, and product managers. There is Integration of AI into Business Functions as Finance, retail, e-commerce, and manufacturing are increasingly adopting AI-based automation to streamline operations, reduce costs, and offer personalized experiences.

Data Scientists analyze large datasets to derive actionable insights, build predictive models, and help organizations make data-driven decisions, with skills in Python, R, SQL, Machine Learning Algorithms, Statistical analysis, Data visualization using Tableau and Power BI, and Deep learning, and are in high demand across healthcare, finance, retail, and e-commerce. Machine Learning Engineers build and deploy machine learning models, implement algorithms, and optimize model performance for real-world applications, with skills in Python, TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch, Neural networks, Cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud, and Algorithm optimization, and are in rising demand, especially in startups and tech giants as companies adopt more AI solutions. AI Research Scientists conduct cutting-edge research in AI, focusing on Deep learning, Reinforcement learning, Computer vision, and NLP, with skills in Machine learning algorithms, Python, Mathematical modeling, Research publications, and Deep learning frameworks, and are in high demand due to growing AI investment from global tech companies and research labs. Data Analysts collect, process, and analyze data to uncover trends, create dashboards, and support decision-making with data-driven insights, with skills in Excel, SQL, Python, Tableau, Power BI, R, and SAS, and are in increasing demand in finance, retail, and healthcare as companies focus on data-driven decision-making. Data Engineers design, construct, and maintain large-scale data pipelines, working with big data technologies to ensure data is stored, processed, and accessible, with skills in SQL, Python, Hadoop, Spark, Kafka, AWS, Google Cloud, and ETL processes, and are in rising demand with the growth of big data and cloud computing in data-driven companies. AI Product Managers oversee the development and deployment of AI-powered products, managing cross-functional teams of engineers and data scientists, with skills in Product lifecycle management, Agile methodology, Machine learning, Project management, and Business strategy, and are in rising demand with AI product development across industries. Business Intelligence (BI) Developers design and develop BI tools to collect, store, and analyze business data, helping businesses make strategic decisions using AI-driven insights, with skills in SQL, Power BI, Tableau, data visualization, reporting tools, and Python, and are in increasing demand as companies shift to data-driven strategies, particularly in e-commerce and retail. Natural Language Processing (NLP) Engineers work on algorithms and models that allow machines to process and understand human language, like chatbots, virtual assistants, and sentiment analysis, with skills in Python, NLP frameworks such as NLTK, SpaCy, and Hugging Face, Machine learning, Deep learning, and Text mining, and are growing in e-commerce, finance, and telecommunications with the rise of AI chatbots, voice assistants, and text analytics.

The following data is from the Key AI and Data Science Job Roles 2025–2030 table: Data Scientists require core skills in Python, R, SQL, Tableau, Power BI, ML, and Deep Learning and are in high demand in Healthcare, Finance, Retail, and E-commerce. Machine Learning Engineers require core skills in Python, TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch, AWS, and Azure and are in high demand in Startups and Tech Giants. AI Research Scientists require core skills in ML Algorithms, Python, Deep Learning frameworks, and NLP and are in high demand in Tech companies and Research Labs. Data Analysts require core skills in Excel, SQL, Python, Tableau, Power BI, R, and SAS and are in high demand in Finance, Retail, and Healthcare. Data Engineers require core skills in SQL, Python, Hadoop, Spark, Kafka, AWS, and ETL and are in high demand in Data-driven Companies and Cloud. AI Product Managers require core skills in Agile, ML, Product Lifecycle, and Project Management and are in high demand in All AI Product Companies. BI Developers require core skills in SQL, Power BI, Tableau, Python, and Reporting Tools and are in high demand in E-commerce and Retail. NLP Engineers require core skills in Python, NLTK, SpaCy, Hugging Face, ML, and Deep Learning and are in high demand in E-commerce, Finance, and Telecom.

The chunk answers questions about the job market for AI and Data Science graduates in India from 2025 to 2030, the role of a Data Scientist and the skills needed, the responsibilities of a Machine Learning Engineer, the role and work of an NLP Engineer, the skills needed for a Data Engineer role, the function of a Business Intelligence Developer, the role of an AI Product Manager, the key drivers of AI employment growth in India, government initiatives supporting AI jobs in India, and how AI startups will affect job opportunities for AIDS graduates.

sections.header: 4. Industry-Specific AI Job Trends, Expected Salary Growth and Required Skills & Certifications
sections.text: The following data is from the Top Companies Recruiting AI/ML Engineers table: Zoho recruited 450 students with a package of 6 LPA. TCS recruited 300 students with a package of 4.5 LPA. Capgemini recruited 250 students with a package of 4 LPA. Wipro recruited 350 students with a package of 3.5 LPA. HCL recruited 200 students with a package of 4 LPA. Accenture recruited 400 students with a package of 4.2 LPA. IBM recruited 150 students with a package of 5 LPA. Tech Mahindra recruited 280 students with a package of 3.8 LPA. Infosys recruited 320 students with a package of 4 LPA. Cognizant recruited 220 students with a package of 4.5 LPA.

sections.header: Industry-Specific AI and Data Science Job Trends:
sections.text: The healthcare sector includes diagnostics, personalized treatment, drug discovery, robot-assisted surgery, bioinformatics, healthcare data analysis, and medical AI applications. Finance and Fintech involves fraud detection, credit scoring, automated trading, and personalized financial services, with employers including banks, insurance companies, and Fintech startups. E-Commerce and Retail uses personalization, dynamic pricing, inventory optimization, and recommendation systems. Automotive and Autonomous Vehicles relies on autonomous driving, vehicle-to-everything (V2X) communication, predictive maintenance, computer vision, sensor data processing, and AI-powered automotive solutions. The telecommunication sector focuses on network optimization, customer service Chatbots, predictive maintenance, 5G, IoT, and Edge computing.

sections.header: Expected Growth in AI and Data Science Jobs:
sections.text:
Job Market Growth: Expected CAGR (Compound Annual Growth Rate) of 30–40%. By 2030, India could see hundreds of thousands of AI and data science job openings in Tech companies, Startups, and Industries adopting data-driven transformation.

Salary Trends: Entry-level roles (0–2 years experience) earn Rs. 6 – Rs. 12 Lakhs per annum. Mid-level roles (3–5 years experience) earn Rs. 12 – Rs. 25 Lakhs per annum. Senior-level roles (5+ years experience) earn Rs. 25 – Rs. 50 Lakhs per annum. AI Research Scientists earn Rs. 30 – Rs. 70 Lakhs per annum.
